author: PC Gamer
text:
I got my hands on Framework's 'MacBook Pro for Linux users' and its tagline isn't just marketing hyperbole
The Framework 13 Pro is a sleek, stylish machine lazer-focused on software devs sick of MacOS.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The blower is back: Cooler Master has made a GPU accessory that pushes hot air outside your case rather than onto your CPU
This thing sits atop of your graphics card and offers a 4–6 degree improvement, says Cooler Master.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cooler Master is playing around with a 2000 W CPU and GPU cooling solution with four huge 180 mm fans
Project AIO Liquid Cooling Solution.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 14 seasonal events schedule: what to expect and when to expect them
All your go-to events under one roof.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Legendary Warhammer artist John Blanche has died
Blanche's work set a stark, brutal vision for the grim dark future.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Which gaming clip just won $500? Check out the best videos of May, featuring nukes in Minecraft, eternal drifts in Forza Horizon 6, and a dragon-riding giant in Skyrim
You sent in some amazing gaming videos in May. Here are our favorites, plus the winner of a $500 gift card.

author: PC Gamer
text:
007 First Light sequels will be 'done by MGM and, theoretically, by Amazon Game Studios,' Amazon exec says
IO Interactive self-published 007 First Light, but don't expect that to happen with future releases.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Summer Game Fest is here, so get ready for a lot of, 'Ugh, that game with the cool trailer used AI'
A pile of new game announcements are coming over the next several days, and we can expect a pile of AI disclosures, too.

author: PC Gamer
text:
868-Back makes hacking as cool as '90s Hollywood thought it was
I just had to hack my own brain to get Back.

author: PC Gamer
text:
They did motion capture on a cube
God of War Laufey necessitated an exciting new form of method acting.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This is me playing Alan Wake 2's native Arm build on an RTX Spark laptop, and I'm here for it
Remarkably, playing Pragmata and Indiana Jones via Prism's emulation wasn't really any different.

author: PC Gamer
text:
For the sixth year running, Dontnod honors Pride Month by giving away an award-winning adventure game
Tell Me Why is free on Steam and Xbox for the duration of June.

author: PC Gamer
text:
So long suckers! I am leaving to drink heavily and repair cheap cars in Poland
In the '90s, specifically.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair announces solid wood front panels for its Frame 5000D PC cases, including a ripple one I'm not entirely sure about
It's a bit trippy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
PlayerUnknown's survival game is shutting down 6 months after launch: 'I have reached the limits of how far I can continue to fund this journey'
PlayerUnknown Productions, founded by Brendan Greene after he left Krafton in 2021, is also laying off staff.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I made treants awkwardly rub shoulders with ringwraiths in the Total War-style RTS roguelike Tabletop Tavern, and I'm surprised as anybody that it worked
It takes all sorts.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Computex 2026 has really highlighted the fact that PC gaming tech isn't all about gaming anymore
Companies seem to be looking to de-niche.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Marathon's new PvE experiment is Bungie's most genius decision yet, so make it a full mode already
Explore the swamp in relative peace.

author: PC Gamer
text:
What's better than one high tier gaming rig? A double-decker gaming rig, according to Thermaltake
Yo dawg.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This new microATX PC case comes with a Venom-like black liquid that reacts to sound and magnets, and I find it weirdly alluring
A case with a different kind of screen.

author: PC Gamer
text:
NAND flash makers earned a record $46 billion in revenues over the first quarter of 2026, a shocking 3.5 times more than last year
Gosh, I wonder how? Was it AI, by any chance?

author: PC Gamer
text:
We got eyes on Ducky's sleek Year of the Horse gaming keyboard, and I can say I like it more than its last horse one
Though the lack of lettering looks like a bit of a mare.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The action-RPG our editor was excited enough to fly to China for just got a 50-day delay
Phantom Blade, uh, 50. Days. Sorry, I should've thought of a joke before I started writing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bungie's traditional launch woes made for a terrible first impression of Marathon's exciting second season and undermined its free-play week
How do all those anteaters, monkeys, or weasels even fit in Bungie HQ?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cherry attempts to change my mind about wireless gaming keyboards with world's first Ultra-Wideband button basher
A clacking good time?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sticking two fingers up at the economy, Biwin's new Origin Code memory sticks are as extreme as you could imagine, with up to 256 GB of DDR5-8000 CL42
Plus an EXPO ULL kit of DDR5-6000 CL26, though none of it is what you'd call affordable.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to watch the PC Gaming Show 2026, including Exodus extended gameplay reveal highlights
Here are all the details on when and where the PC Gaming Show premieres this year from worldwide times to streaming platforms.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Morrowind modder returns to remake a 20-year-old epic—a beloved 'Discworld, not Monty Python' project that's 'unexpectedly lore-friendly'
Rise and shine.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Thermaltake has built a PSU that splits in two… on purpose
All in the name of an easier installation process.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair's new and supposedly affordable seat continues 'the move away from traditional gaming chair style'
I'm liking the vision for this one.
